name: Obiols Trail
description: Ride the Ruta d'Obiols circular mountain biking trail, a moderate 12.1-mile route with 1003 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Berga, nestled at the foothills of the Catalan Pyrenees, offers a diverse landscape blending mountains and plains. The region is characterized by majestic mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and lush forests, with a significant portion within the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró. Hikers can explore a variety of trails that traverse these natural features, including the iconic Pedraforca Mountain and the panoramic Queralt mountain range. Best hiking trails around Berga The most popular hiking route is Llobregat Greenway and Sant Quirze de Pedret Loop, a 9.5 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete, following the Llobregat river and passing historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Queralt Sanctuary – Font Negra loop from Berga, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This route leads to the significant Queralt Sanctuary, offering expansive views over the valleys. Local hikers also love the Troballa Cave – Queralt – Queralt Sanctuary loop from Berga, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through the Queralt mountain range and past the Troballa Cave, often completed in about 5 hours 51 minutes. Hiking around Berga is defined by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and the presence of the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, offering a wide variety of alpine scenery. The network provides options for various ability levels, from leisurely walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Berga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Berga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I find around Berga?
A: Berga offers a wide variety of hiking trails, from leisurely walks to challenging ascents. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and lush forests, with a significant portion within the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró. You'll find routes suitable for various ability levels, including paths along rivers and trails leading to significant viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Berga?
A: Yes, Berga has several easy and family-friendly options. For a leisurely walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950321277/llobregat-greenway-and-sant-quirze-de-pedret-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat Greenway and Sant Quirze de Pedret Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Llobregat river and passes historical sites. There are 13 easy tours available in the area, perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed outing.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can visit while hiking in Berga?
A: Many trails in Berga lead to stunning landmarks and offer panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, perched at 1,200 meters, is a significant spiritual site and an exceptional viewpoint. You can reach it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38189714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary – Font Negra loop from Berga&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the iconic Pedraforca Mountain and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/840166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rasos de Peguera&lt;/a&gt; area, known for its natural beauty and historical remnants.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Berga?
A: Yes, circular routes are popular in Berga. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary – Troballa Cave – Queralt loop from Berga&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge and takes you through the Queralt mountain range. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950321277/llobregat-greenway-and-sant-quirze-de-pedret-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat Greenway and Sant Quirze de Pedret Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate path following the river.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Berga?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Berga are spring and autumn. During these times, the climate is mild, and the natural colors are vibrant, creating an ideal setting for outdoor exploration. While summer is also possible, it can be warmer, and winter might bring snow to higher elevations, especially in the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Berga?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Berga region. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Parc Natural del Cadí-Moixeró, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check specific trail regulations if you plan to visit a nature reserve.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in the Berga area?
A: There are over 75 hiking trails available around Berga, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 13 easy routes, 58 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring options for every skill level.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Berga?
A: The hiking routes in Berga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views from places like the Queralt Sanctuary, and the well-maintained paths that lead through both mountainous landscapes and serene river valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Berga?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, Berga offers challenging routes, particularly within the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park. The ascent of Pedraforca Mountain, for instance, presents demanding trails with steep inclines. A difficult option from our selection is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10470395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Troballa Cave – Queralt – Queralt Sanctuary loop from Berga&lt;/a&gt;, which involves significant elevation gain.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Berga?
A: While Berga itself is accessible by public transport, direct public transport options to specific remote trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to smaller villages or key starting points. For many trails, especially those deeper in the mountains, driving or arranging local transport might be necessary.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Berga?
A: Parking is generally available at popular trailheads and near significant attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;. In Berga town, you'll find various parking areas. For more remote starting points, look for designated parking spots in nearby villages or small clearings, but always ensure you are parking legally and considerately.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the hiking trails in Berga?
A: Berga town center offers a good selection of cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. While fewer options are directly on the trails, some popular spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; may have facilities or nearby establishments. It's always a good idea to pack snacks and water, especially for longer routes.
tips: A curious place to see the tombs on the ground.
A beautiful little waterfall next to an old mill. This is a good place for a photo but not for swimming or picnicking because the farm next door is private.
This church is a Romanesque construction from the middle of the 12th century. I think the original altar is in the MNAC in Barcelona. This is a place to see on a hike or mountain bike in this area.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta d'Obiols - circular suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: The Ruta d'Obiols - circular is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. This means it's best suited for riders with some experience, offering a balance of physical exertion and technical challenge without being overly demanding. Beginners might find certain sections challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this exact route are limited, mountain biking in this region typically involves diverse landscapes. You can expect a mix of forest tracks, potentially some technical singletrack, and areas with rocky paths. The route offers a blend of natural beauty and varied riding surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You'll encounter the historic Sant Vicenç d'Obiols Hermitage, the scenic Bellús Mill Waterfall, and the beautiful Santa Maria d'Avià.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta d'Obiols - circular trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Ruta d'Obiols - circular trailhead is not provided in the available data. It is advisable to check local maps or community forums for the most current parking options near the starting point of the route.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Information regarding public transport options directly to the trailhead of the Ruta d'Obiols - circular is not available. Planning for personal vehicle access is recommended, or researching local bus services to nearby towns and then cycling to the start.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Ruta d'Obiols - circular?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route generally depends on local weather patterns. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and stable conditions for mountain biking. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder weather or even snow at higher elevations, making some sections less accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta d'Obiols - circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, specific regulations for the Ruta d'Obiols - circular are not detailed. It's generally a good practice to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users, and to always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Ruta d'Obiols - circular. Most natural trails in the area are freely accessible, but it's always good to be aware of any local regulations that might apply.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The available data does not specify cafes or facilities directly along the Ruta d'Obiols - circular. However, as the route passes through or near towns like Avià, you are likely to find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and shops there. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta d'Obiols - circular intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Sender Històric: Catalunya, Sender dels Maquis, TransCatalunya. Catalunya Central - Pirineus, Camí Ral de Manresa a Berga, Camí del Llobregat, PR-C 144 Ruta de les Colònies en BTT, Ruta de les Colònies, Camí d'Avià a Vilamarí, and Camí de Graugés a l'Ametlla de Casserres.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta d'Obiols - circular suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and approximately 19.4 km length, the route might be challenging for very young children or inexperienced family members. It is more suitable for families with older children who have some mountain biking experience and good stamina.
